responses.note: LLM's first attempt — comma-separates the two effect clauses (`with reads(UserDB), reads(OrderDB)`), a universal list-comma habit carried over from every mainstream language. Kāra effect clauses are space-separated; the only defect is the stray comma, which karac reports with a machine-applicable delete-the-comma replacement that `karac fix` applies in one pass. Converges as clean-after-karac-fix.
